protocol.name: wlr_layer_shell_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright © 2017 Drew DeVault Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_shell_v1

description.summary: create surfaces that are layers of the desktop

interface.description: Clients can use this interface to assign the surface_layer role to wl_surfaces. Such surfaces are assigned to a "layer" of the output and rendered with a defined z-depth respective to each other. They may also be anchored to the edges and corners of a screen and specify input handling semantics. This interface should be suitable for the implementation of many desktop shell components, and a broad number of different use cases.

entry.name: already_constructed
entry.summary: wl_surface has a buffer attached or committed

request.name: get_layer_surface

description.summary: create a layer_surface from a surface

request.description: Create a layer surface for an existing surface. This assigns the role of layer_surface, or raises a protocol error if another role is already assigned. Creating a layer surface from a wl_surface which has a buffer attached or committed is a client error, and any attempts by a client to attach or manipulate a buffer prior to the first layer_surface.configure call must also be treated as errors. You may pass NULL for output to allow the compositor to decide which output to use. Generally this will be the one that the user most recently interacted with. Clients can specify a namespace that defines the purpose of the layer surface.

arg.name: layer
arg.summary: layer to add this surface to

request.name: destroy
request.description: This request indicates that the client will not use the layer_shell object any more. Objects that have been created through this instance are not affected.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_surface_v1
interface.description: An interface that may be implemented by a wl_surface, for surfaces that are designed to be rendered as a layer of a stacked desktop-like environment. Layer surface state (layer, size, anchor, exclusive zone, margin, interactivity) is double-buffered, and will be applied at the time wl_surface.commit of the corresponding wl_surface is called.

entry.name: invalid_exclusive_edge
entry.summary: exclusive edge is invalid given the surface anchors

entry.name: top
entry.summary: the top edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: bottom
entry.summary: the bottom edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: left
entry.summary: the left edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: right
entry.summary: the right edge of the anchor rectangle

request.name: set_size

description.summary: sets the size of the surface

request.description: Sets the size of the surface in surface-local coordinates. The compositor will display the surface with the given size. If you pass 0 for either value, the compositor will assign it and inform you of the assignment in the configure event. You must set your anchor to opposite edges in the dimensions you specify 0 in.

request.name: set_anchor

description.summary: configures the anchor point of the surface

request.description: Requests that the compositor anchor the surface to the specified edges and corners. If two orthogonal edges are specified (e.g. 'top' and 'left'), then the anchor point will be the intersection of the edges (e.g. the top left corner of the output); otherwise the anchor point will be centered on that edge, or in the center of the output if none is specified.

request.name: set_exclusive_zone

description.summary: configures the exclusive geometry of this surface

request.description: Requests that the compositor avoids occluding an area with other surfaces. The compositor's use of this information is implementation-dependent - do not assume that this region will not actually be occluded. A positive value is only meaningful if the surface is anchored to one edge or an edge and both perpendicular edges. If the surface is not anchored, anchored to more than one edge (but not anchored to exactly one edge and both perpendicular edges), or does not have an exclusive zone set, the exclusive zone will be ignored. The exclusive zone value specifies how far from the edge of the output the surface will request that content not be placed. When placed positive, the layer surface itself is excluded from this region and its geometry is unchanged; When placed 0, the surface indicates that it would like to not be occluded. A negative value means the surface does not wish to make any surfaces below it occlude those above, and is the default value. Providing a larger exclusive zone than the surface's bounds will behave as if the surface is placed at the edge of the output. See anchor for details of what edge means.

request.name: set_margin

description.summary: sets a margin from the anchor point

request.description: Requests that the surface be placed some distance away from the anchor point on the output, in surface-local coordinates. Setting this value for edges you are not anchored to has no effect.

request.name: set_keyboard_interactivity
request.description: Set how keyboard events are delivered to this surface. By default, layer shell surfaces do not receive keyboard events; this setting is persisted in the surface state. If focus_follows_pointer is set to true, the compositor may deliver keyboard events to the surface whenever a pointer is over it; otherwise keyboard events are delivered according to the set interactivity mode. There are currently three options: none: This is the default option. This value indicates that this surface is not interested in keyboard events, and the compositor should never assign it the keyboard focus. As this is the default, clients do not need to set this property if they do not want to receive keyboard events. exclusive: Requests that the compositor send all keyboard events to this layer. If used interactively, clients should use this option sparingly and only when they need guaranteed keyboard focus, for example in a lock screen situation. Any surface which accidentally set this on any other surface will likely be considered a malicious client. on_demand: This requests the compositor to allow this surface to be focused and unfocused by the user in an implementation-defined manner. Interactive shell components using this option should be designed to allow the user to navigate to and from the surface using a keyboard. e.g. this can be used to implement a tabbed panel where the user can switch focus between an scrapbook, a media player, and a chat application.

request.name: ack_configure
request.description: When a configure event is received, if a client commits the surface in response to the configure event, then the client must make an ack_configure request sometime before the commit request, passing along the serial of the configure event. If the client receives multiple configure events before it can respond to one, it only has to ack the last configure event.

arg.name: serial
arg.summary: the serial from the configure event

request.name: set_layer

description.summary: change the layer of the surface

request.description: Change the layer that the surface is rendered on. Layer is double-buffered, see wl_surface.commit.

arg.name: layer
arg.summary: layer to move this surface to

event.name: configure
event.description: The configure event asks the client to resize its surface. Clients should arrange their surface for the new states, and then send an ack_configure request with the serial sent in this configure event at some point before committing the new surface. The client is free to dismiss all but the last configure event it received. The width and height arguments specify the size of the window in surface-local coordinates. The size is a hint, in the sense that the client is free to ignore it if it doesn't resize, pick a smaller size (to satisfy aspect ratio or resize in steps of NxM pixels). If the client picks a smaller size and is anchored to two opposite anchors (e.g. 'top' and 'bottom'), the surface will be centered on this axis. If the width or height arguments are zero, it means the client should decide its own window dimension. This may happen when the compositor needs to configure the state of the surface but doesn't have any information about any previous or expected dimension.

event.name: closed
event.description: The closed event is sent by the compositor when the surface will no longer be shown. The output may have been destroyed or the user may have asked for it to be removed. Further changes to the surface will be ignored. The client should destroy the resource after receiving this event, and create a new surface if they so choose.
